# Ingest Existing AWS Account

This runbook automates the process of ingesting an existing AWS account into your Gruntwork Reference Architecture. Instead of manually updating `accounts.yml`, creating account request files, importing into Control Tower, and scaffolding boilerplate — this runbook handles it all in a guided workflow.

**What this runbook does:**

1. Validates your local environment and AWS authentication
2. Collects details about the existing account to ingest
3. Registers the account in Control Tower (via the management account)
4. Creates an S3 bucket in the target account for Terraform state
5. Scaffolds the account baseline configuration (directory structure, Terragrunt configs)
6. Generates a PR-ready changeset

---

## Pre-flight Checks

Before we begin, let's make sure your environment is set up correctly.

<Check
  id="check-aws-cli"
  command="aws --version"
  title="Check if AWS CLI is installed"
  description="The [AWS CLI](https://aws.amazon.com/cli/) is required to interact with AWS services."
  successMessage="AWS CLI is installed!"
  failMessage="AWS CLI is not installed. Please [install it](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/getting-started-install.html) to continue."
/>

<Check
  id="check-jq"
  command="jq --version"
  title="Check if `jq` is installed"
  description="`jq` is used for parsing JSON responses from AWS API calls."
  successMessage="`jq` is installed!"
  failMessage="`jq` is not installed. Please install it to continue."
/>

---

## Authenticate to AWS (Management Account)

Authenticate to your **AWS management account**. This is the account that manages your AWS Organization and Control Tower. We'll use these credentials to register the existing account into your organization.

<AwsAuth
  id="mgmt-account-auth"
  title="Authenticate to Management Account"
  description="Authenticate to the AWS management (root) account that manages your AWS Organization."
  defaultRegion="us-east-1"
/>

Let's verify the management account authentication and confirm the account identity.

<Check
  id="verify-mgmt-auth"
  path="checks/verify-mgmt-account.sh"
  title="Verify Management Account Identity"
  description="Confirms you're authenticated to an AWS management account with the right permissions."
  awsAuthId="mgmt-account-auth"
  successMessage="Authenticated to management account!"
  failMessage="Authentication failed or this doesn't appear to be a management account."
/>

---

## Existing Account Details

Provide the details of the existing AWS account you want to ingest into your Reference Architecture.

<Inputs id="account-details">
```yaml
variables:
  - name: AccountName
    type: string
    description: >
      A human-readable name for this account (e.g., "prod-workloads", "data-analytics").
      This will be used as the directory name in your infrastructure-live repo.
    validations:
      - required
    x-section: Account Information

  - name: AccountId
    type: string
    description: >
      The 12-digit AWS account ID of the existing account to ingest
      (e.g., "123456789012").
    validations:
      - required
    x-section: Account Information

  - name: AccountEmail
    type: string
    description: >
      The root email address associated with this AWS account.
    validations:
      - required
    x-section: Account Information

  - name: AccountEnvironment
    type: enum
    description: >
      The environment this account belongs to. This determines which
      baseline configurations and guardrails are applied.
    options:
      - dev
      - stage
      - prod
      - sandbox
      - shared
    default: dev
    x-section: Account Configuration

  - name: DefaultRegion
    type: enum
    description: The primary AWS region for this account's resources.
    options:
      - us-east-1
      - us-east-2
      - us-west-1
      - us-west-2
      - eu-west-1
      - eu-west-2
      - eu-central-1
      - ap-southeast-1
      - ap-northeast-1
    default: us-east-1
    x-section: Account Configuration

  - name: OrgNamePrefix
    type: string
    description: >
      Your organization name prefix, used for naming conventions
      (e.g., "acme" for acme-dev, acme-prod).
    default: "acme"
    x-section: Account Configuration
```
</Inputs>

---

## Register Account in Control Tower

Now we'll register the existing account in your AWS Control Tower setup. This simulates updating `accounts.yml` and creating the `_new-account-requests/account-xxx.yml` file, then importing the account into the `control-tower-multi-account-factory`.

<Command
  id="register-account-control-tower"
  path="scripts/register-account.sh"
  title="Register Account in Control Tower"
  description="Simulates registering the existing account in AWS Control Tower by updating the account registry and creating a request file."
  inputsId="account-details"
  awsAuthId="mgmt-account-auth"
  successMessage="Account **{{ .inputs.AccountName }}** ({{ .inputs.AccountId }}) registered in Control Tower!"
  failMessage="Failed to register the account. Check the logs for details."
/>

---

## Authenticate to Target Account

Now authenticate to the **target account** — the existing account being ingested. We'll create foundational resources (like an S3 state bucket) directly in this account.

<AwsAuth
  id="target-account-auth"
  title="Authenticate to Target Account"
  description="Authenticate to the existing AWS account you are ingesting (Account ID: the one you entered above)."
  defaultRegion="us-east-1"
/>

<Check
  id="verify-target-auth"
  path="checks/verify-target-account.sh"
  title="Verify Target Account Identity"
  description="Confirms you're authenticated to the correct target account."
  inputsId="account-details"
  awsAuthId="target-account-auth"
  successMessage="Authenticated to target account {{ .inputs.AccountId }}!"
  failMessage="Authentication failed or account ID doesn't match. Expected {{ .inputs.AccountId }}."
/>

---

## Create Terraform State Bucket

Every account in the Reference Architecture needs an S3 bucket for Terraform/OpenTofu remote state. Let's create one in the target account.

<Command
  id="create-state-bucket"
  path="scripts/create-state-bucket.sh"
  title="Create Terraform State S3 Bucket"
  description="Creates a versioned, encrypted S3 bucket for storing Terraform/OpenTofu state in the target account."
  inputsId="account-details"
  awsAuthId="target-account-auth"
  successMessage="State bucket created successfully!"
  failMessage="Failed to create the state bucket. Check the logs for details."
/>

<Check
  id="verify-state-bucket"
  path="checks/verify-state-bucket.sh"
  title="Verify State Bucket"
  description="Confirms the S3 state bucket was created and is properly configured."
  inputsId="account-details"
  awsAuthId="target-account-auth"
  successMessage="State bucket is configured correctly with versioning and encryption!"
  failMessage="State bucket verification failed."
/>

---

## Create Tags Bucket

We'll also create an S3 bucket to demonstrate tagging standards for the ingested account.

<Command
  id="create-tags-bucket"
  path="scripts/create-tags-bucket.sh"
  title="Create Tags Standard S3 Bucket"
  description="Creates an S3 bucket with your organization's standard tags applied, demonstrating tagging governance."
  inputsId="account-details"
  awsAuthId="target-account-auth"
  successMessage="Tags bucket created with standard organization tags!"
  failMessage="Failed to create the tags bucket."
/>

---

## Scaffold Account Baseline

Now let's generate the boilerplate configuration files for this account. This creates the directory structure, Terragrunt config files, and account-level settings that match a freshly vended account.

### Preview: Account Configuration File

Here's what the `.gruntwork/account.hcl` configuration will look like for this account:

<TemplateInline id="account-hcl-preview" inputsId="account-details" outputPath="{{ .inputs.AccountName }}/.gruntwork/account.hcl" generateFile={true}>
```hcl
# Account configuration for {{ .inputs.AccountName }}
# Ingested from existing account {{ .inputs.AccountId }}

locals {
  account_name = "{{ .inputs.AccountName }}"
  account_id   = "{{ .inputs.AccountId }}"
  account_email = "{{ .inputs.AccountEmail }}"
  environment  = "{{ .inputs.AccountEnvironment }}"
  default_region = "{{ .inputs.DefaultRegion }}"
}
```
</TemplateInline>

### Preview: Account Terragrunt Config

<TemplateInline id="account-terragrunt-preview" inputsId="account-details" outputPath="{{ .inputs.AccountName }}/account.hcl" generateFile={true}>
```hcl
# Account-level Terragrunt configuration for {{ .inputs.AccountName }}
# This file is included in all Terragrunt configurations within this account.

locals {
  account_name = "{{ .inputs.AccountName }}"
  account_id   = "{{ .inputs.AccountId }}"
  environment  = "{{ .inputs.AccountEnvironment }}"
}
```
</TemplateInline>

### Preview: Account Entry for `accounts.yml`

<TemplateInline id="accounts-yml-entry" inputsId="account-details" outputPath="_new-account-requests/{{ .inputs.AccountName }}.yml" generateFile={true}>
```yaml
# Account request for {{ .inputs.AccountName }}
# Generated by the Ingest Existing AWS Account runbook

account_name: {{ .inputs.AccountName }}
account_id: "{{ .inputs.AccountId }}"
account_email: {{ .inputs.AccountEmail }}
environment: {{ .inputs.AccountEnvironment }}
default_region: {{ .inputs.DefaultRegion }}
org_name_prefix: {{ .inputs.OrgNamePrefix }}
status: ingested
ingested_at: "{{ now | date "2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00" }}"
```
</TemplateInline>

### Preview: Region-level Terragrunt Config

<TemplateInline id="region-hcl-preview" inputsId="account-details" outputPath="{{ .inputs.AccountName }}/{{ .inputs.DefaultRegion }}/region.hcl" generateFile={true}>
```hcl
# Region-level Terragrunt configuration for {{ .inputs.DefaultRegion }}

locals {
  aws_region = "{{ .inputs.DefaultRegion }}"
}
```
</TemplateInline>

---

## Verify Account Ingestion

Let's run a final set of checks to make sure everything was set up correctly.

<Check
  id="verify-generated-files"
  command="ls -la $GENERATED_FILES/"
  title="Verify Generated Files"
  description="Confirms that all the account baseline files were generated."
  successMessage="All account baseline files are present!"
  failMessage="Some files appear to be missing."
/>

<Check
  id="verify-resources-in-target"
  path="checks/verify-resources.sh"
  title="Verify AWS Resources in Target Account"
  description="Confirms that the S3 buckets were created in the target account."
  inputsId="account-details"
  awsAuthId="target-account-auth"
  successMessage="All AWS resources verified in account {{ .inputs.AccountId }}!"
  failMessage="Some resources could not be verified."
/>

---

## Clean Up (Optional)

If you want to remove the demo resources created in the target account, run the cleanup command below.

<Command
  id="cleanup-resources"
  path="scripts/cleanup-resources.sh"
  title="Clean Up Demo Resources"
  description="Removes the S3 buckets created during this runbook. Only run this if you don't need the resources."
  inputsId="account-details"
  awsAuthId="target-account-auth"
  successMessage="Demo resources cleaned up!"
  failMessage="Cleanup encountered errors. You may need to manually remove some resources."
/>

---

## Next Steps

Congratulations! You've ingested the existing account **{{ .inputs.AccountName }}** into your Reference Architecture.

Here's what to do next:

1. **Review the generated files** in the file panel and download them
2. **Copy the files** into your `infrastructure-live-root` repository
3. **Create a pull request** with the `[skip ci]` tag to avoid triggering pipelines prematurely
4. **Run `terragrunt plan`** in the new account directory to verify the baseline
5. **Apply the baseline** once the plan looks correct

> **Tip:** Use the `[skip ci]` commit message tag when pushing these changes, since Pipelines will treat this as a large batch of events otherwise.
